{{Quote|Your targets are going up against the meanest franchise players we've got—and we've provided an extra edge! We've removed the safeties from the Togorian sisters' weapons and the Tyluun Night-Soarer's claws have been soaked with venom.|Bardron to Jervo Thalien at the Tandem Open finals|<ref name="KOTOR41" />}}
The '''Night-Soarers''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tyluun]]. They were bipedal in form, and large wings extended from their [[Bone/Legends|ribcages]] and [[Limb/Legends|arms]], making them capable of independent flight. Night-Soarers earned a reputation in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] as [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] through their stealth and cunning. Some Night-Soarers participated in gladiatorial games, such as the [[Tandem Open]] on [[Jervo's World]] or [[Death/Legends|death]] matches aboard [[The Wheel/Legends|The Wheel]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Night-Soarers were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]], of either blue<ref name="KOTOR41" /> or brown skin pigmentation.<ref name="KOTOR40" /> They had pointed [[Ear/Legends|ears]] and a bald head with a central [[Bone/Legends|cranial]] projection.<ref name="Marvel22" /> The Night-Soarers were bipedal in form, and their [[Limb/Legends|arms]] ended in five-fingered hands that were tipped with sharp claws.<ref name="KOTOR41" /> Their most distinguishing characteristic was a pair of membranous wings formed by webbing that extended from their ribcages. For some Night-Soarers, these wings extended out to about the elbow,<ref name="Marvel22" /> whereas for others, the wings extended well beyond their wrists. These wings were strong enough for the Night-Soarers to achieve independent flight.<ref name="KOTOR41" />

Some Night-Soarers wore garments only on their lower half,<ref name="KOTOR40" /><ref name="Marvel22" /> while others also wore armless tunics. They were capable of speaking [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]].<ref name="KOTOR41" />

==History==
===Galactic Republic===
{{Quote|You're through, Goethar! ''[…]''Wait! Don't speed up—you'll kill us both.|A Night-Soarer attacks Goethar Kleej|<ref name="KOTOR41" />}}
[[File:Tyluun_vs_Goethar.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A Night-Soarer attacks Goethar Kleej.]]
The Night-Soarers originated from [[Tyluun]], a gloomy [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Juvex sector/Legends|Juvex sector]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> They were part of the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] by as early as [[3963 BBY]]. In that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], two Night-Soarers competed in the [[Tandem Open]] on [[Jervo's World]], a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] orbiting [[Pantolomin/Legends|Pantolomin]]. The Tandem Open was one of the feature events at the station, in which all competitors were airborne; working in pairs, they attempted to defeat all other opponents using non-lethal weapons, until only two participants were left standing in each heat. Those two survivors then progressed through to the next round. One of the Night-Soarer players was knocked out in the semi-final round while trying to eliminate two other players—a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] going by the name of "[[Rohlan Dyre|Spikes]]," and [[Goethar Kleej]], a [[Gotal/Legends|Gotal]] veteran of the competition—who were fighting each other on the ground. The Night-Soarer and his [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]]-using partner approached them from the air, believing them to be too distracted to notice their arrival; however, Goethar and Spikes saw them coming and shot them both out of the air, eliminating them from the event.<ref name="KOTOR40" />

The second Night-Soarer—considered to be among the meanest [[Franchise]] players in the competition—survived through to the final. For that round, the organizers had a vested interest in ensuring certain finalists were eliminated: Goethar and his son [[Aubin Kleej|Aubin]], who was trying to expose the inhumane training and recruitment practices behind the franchise running the tournament, as well as Spikes and [[Zayne Carrick]], against whom one of the organizers, [[Jervo Thalien]], carried a grudge. In order to facilitate those players' elimination, the organizers soaked the Night-Soarer's claws in venom. He then proceeded to attack Goethar directly while the Gotal was riding his [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]]. However, when Goethar accelerated into his attacker, the Night-Soarer was pinned to the front of the vehicle as Goethar crashed through one of the windows overlooking the course. The Night-Soarer was eliminated from the Tandem Open, with Spikes eventually declared the winner.<ref name="KOTOR41" />

===Republic Dark Age===
[[File:NightSoarers-Escape3.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Malakite's Night-Soarers during the Republic Dark Age]]
During the [[Republic Dark Age]], large numbers of Night-Soarers served in [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Malakite]]'s [[The Menagerie|Menagerie]] forces. The technology-hating Sith Lord bred them in large numbers since his strategy involved using large waves of organic troops to overwhelm technologically-superior enemies. They saw action during the [[Massacre on Skarpos|Second Battle of Skarpos]] where they ferried Malakite's [[Mutate]] soldiers.<ref name="SWKE13">[[Knight Errant: Escape 3|''Knight Errant: Escape'' 3]]</ref>

===Galactic Empire===
[[File:Night-soarer_gladiator.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A Night-Soarer competing on The Wheel.]]
{{Quote|Okay, okay. Stop gloating champ. I'm the one who signalled you to turn and fire!|Han Solo to Chewbacca|<ref name="Marvel 23">[[Star Wars (1977) 23|''Star Wars'' (1977) 23]]</ref>}}
By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Night-Soarers had earned a reputation throughout the galaxy as cunning and stealthy [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]],<ref name="Marvel22" /> making them invaluable to the criminal underworld.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|271|}}</ref> In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], a Night-Soarer participated in a [[Death/Legends|death]] duel aboard [[The Wheel/Legends|The Wheel]],<ref name="Marvel22" /> a space station and gambling haven located in the [[Besh Gorgon system/Legends|Besh Gorgon system]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="Elusive adventure">{{WEGCite|story=Elusive (adventure)|book=No Disintegrations (West End Games)}}</ref> Held in a zero [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] arena filled with explosive planetoids, the Night-Soarer was one of the last four surviving gladiators. Knowing that two of the other competitors—a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and his [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] companion, [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]]—would work together, the Night-Soarer hid behind a planetoid, waiting to strike when they were committed to attacking other opponents.<ref name="Marvel22" /> When it appeared that Chewbacca had struck down Solo, the Night-Soarer moved in on the Wookiee from behind, wanting to get close enough to ensure a clean kill. His plan failed, however, when Solo—only faking his own death—signaled to the Wookiee that the Night-Soarer was approaching. Chewbacca spun around, shooting the Night-Soarer with his blaster and killing him.<ref name="Marvel 23" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Night-Soarer species first appeared in two successive issues of the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'' comic]] series published by [[Marvel Comics]] in [[1979]]. The two issues, [[Star Wars (1977) 22|''Star Wars'' (1977) 22]] and [[Star Wars (1977) 23|''Star Wars'' (1977) 23]], were written [[Archie Goodwin]] and penciled by [[Carmine Infantino]]. Following this, the Night-Soarers were absent from Star Wars continuity for almost 30 years, until receiving an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', which clarified the correct capitalization; prior to this the species had only been spelled all in capitals.<ref name="CSWE" />

The next year, Night-Soarers appeared in two successive issues of the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Knights of the Old Republic'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic books]] published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] in [[2009]]. These two issues, [[Knights of the Old Republic 40|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 40]] and [[Knights of the Old Republic 41|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 41]], were written by [[John Jackson Miller]] and penciled by [[Brian Ching]]. In his production notes for issue 41, Miller reveals that this story arc was in part a tribute to the work of Goodwin, hence the re-appearance of the Night-Soarers as well as other elements such as a [[Mandallian Giant]] and the spectator riot that occurs at the close of the comic.<ref name="Faraway-''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #41''">{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor41.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #41''|author=[[John Jackson Miller]]|date=2009-05-22}}</ref>

Night-Soarers also appeared in [[Knight Errant: Escape 3|''Knight Errant: Escape'' 3]] and [[Knight Errant: Escape 4|''Knight Errant: Escape'' 4]], the thirteenth and fourteenth issues of ''[[Star Wars: Knight Errant: Escape]]'', which were released on [[August 8]] and [[September 12]] [[2012]]. In this story arc, they appeared as minions of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Malakite]], a lesser antagonist within the series. Their story plot was also written by Miller and they were penciled by [[Marco Castiello]].<ref name="SWKE13" /><ref name="SWKE14">[[Knight Errant: Escape 4|''Knight Errant: Escape'' 4]]</ref>
